page: Microsoft Azure EC4as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECASv5 server family. Powered by dedicated AMD processors, this instance provides 4 dedicated virtual CPUs and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include bundled local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC4as_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive CPU scaling or local scratch disks. The hardware profile is designed for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics tasks that benefit from a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure EC4as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include bundled local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads due to its high resource density of RAM relative to compute cores. The EC4as_v5 is structured for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity without the overhead of high CPU core counts or local storage.
